evidence.raw_snippet:
PubMed ID: 21552063. 2011/06/01 00:00. BACKGROUND: Anal manometry is routinely used in the assessment of the anal 
sphincters in patients with fecal incontinence or suspected sphincter injury. 
Such physiological information is complementary to the anatomical assessment 
provided by anal endosonography. The evolution of 3-dimensional anal 
endosonography provides more diagnostically useful information in complex cases.
